name: SolarisLDAP User Account Lockout Detected
description: Detects a user account lockout event in Solaris LDAP. Account lockouts can be a sign of an adversary attempting to brute-force a password, a legitimate user with a forgotten password, or a misconfigured service attempting to authenticate with incorrect credentials. This is a common reconnaissance and defense evasion technique.

prompts.prompt: An alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: a user account, **{user}**, has been locked out in Solaris LDAP. This event is a strong indicator of a **Brute-Force Attack** or an attempt to bypass security controls. Immediate investigation is required to determine the cause of the lockout and to secure the affected account. The event occurred at **{log_ts}**.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The name of the user account that was locked out.
